messages.content: You output JSON for task plans.

You are a senior project planner producing structured task plans.

Follow this JSON schema:
TaskPlan { originalPrompt: string; subtasks: Subtask[]; totalSubtasks: number; decompositionStrategy?: string }
Subtask { id: string (kebab-case), title: string, description: string (>=10 chars), status: one of pending|in_progress|completed|failed, dependencies: string[], estimatedComplexity: low|medium|high, successCriteria: string }
Constraints: 2-10 subtasks, actionable descriptions, include logical dependencies, avoid circular references, IDs unique.
Original prompt: Create a small library with one function and a unit test.
Clarifications:
None
Previous validation issues:
None
Respond ONLY with JSON matching the schema.

response:
{
  "originalPrompt": "Create a small library with one function and a unit test.",
  "subtasks": [
    {
      "id": "define-spec",
      "title": "Define function specification",
      "description": "Write a short specification describing the library purpose, function name, input/output types and edge cases to handle.",
      "status": "in_progress",
      "dependencies": [],
      "estimatedComplexity": "low",
      "successCriteria": "A one-page spec or markdown file exists that names the function, documents parameters and return value, and lists at least three edge cases."
    },
    {
      "id": "init-project-structure",
      "title": "Initialize project structure",
      "description": "Create repository layout, package manifest, source and test directories, and a basic build/test config.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "define-spec"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "low",
      "successCriteria": "Repository contains package manifest (e.g., package.json or pyproject.toml), src/ and tests/ directories, and a minimal test runner config file."
    },
    {
      "id": "implement-core-function",
      "title": "Implement the library function",
      "description": "Write the single function implementation according to the spec, include input validation and inline comments.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "init-project-structure",
        "define-spec"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "medium",
      "successCriteria": "Source file contains the function with documented parameters and returns correct outputs for the spec's example inputs."
    },
    {
      "id": "write-unit-test",
      "title": "Create unit test for the function",
      "description": "Implement unit tests that cover normal cases, edge cases from the spec, and at least one failure case.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "implement-core-function"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "medium",
      "successCriteria": "Tests file exists with assertions for normal and edge cases and can be executed by the configured test runner."
    },
    {
      "id": "run-tests-and-fix",
      "title": "Run tests and fix issues",
      "description": "Execute the unit tests, iterate on function or tests until all tests pass and fix lint or runtime errors.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "write-unit-test",
        "implement-core-function"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "medium",
      "successCriteria": "All tests pass with zero failures; any discovered bugs are fixed and regression tests added if needed."
    },
    {
      "id": "create-readme",
      "title": "Add README with usage and test instructions",
      "description": "Write a README that explains the function purpose, shows example usage, installation and how to run tests.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "implement-core-function",
        "write-unit-test"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "low",
      "successCriteria": "README.md contains usage examples, installation steps, and a clear command to run the unit tests."
    }
  ],
  "totalSubtasks": 6,
  "decompositionStrategy": "Top-down: start by defining the function specification, initialize the project scaffolding, implement the function, add tests, run and fix tests, then finalize documentation."
}
